nodes.label: mshell UI Design Language (DESIGN.md)
nodes.rationale: The binding spec for every mshell bar pill, menu, and dashboard tile. Goal: every surface reads as one coherent system. Margo adopts GNOME HIG/libadwaita metrics + component anatomy; identity lives in matugen adaptivity, the severity ladder, keyboard-first interaction, and layer-shell surfaces, not in bespoke geometry.

nodes.label: design tokens (never hardcode)
nodes.rationale: Two non-overlapping corner systems: the fixed Adwaita scale for widget interiors, and config-driven --radius-widget/--radius-window only for the frame/bar chrome. Component geometry lives once in _components.scss; primitives read only tokens, widgets read primitives. Spacing snaps to 4/8/12/16/24/32/48.

nodes.label: severity ladder (calm/warn/danger)
nodes.rationale: Rust computes the class name, SCSS owns the colour. --warning (#e0af68) and --success (#9ece6a) are wallpaper-independent (matugen never re-declares these keys) so status signals stay recognisable regardless of palette. Thresholds tuned high so idle reads calm.

nodes.label: active-state tinting (--primary)
nodes.rationale: Active = primary tint, never a separate badge or size change. Rust toggles a CSS class, SCSS paints it.

nodes.label: bar pill contract
nodes.rationale: Root gtk::Box with ok-button-surface/ok-bar-widget classes; wrap clickable content in an ok-button-flat button (don't paint your own pill background). Left-click opens the menu, right-click cycles an ephemeral in-memory display mode. Symbolic icons only.

nodes.label: layer-shell menu (card chrome)
nodes.rationale: Menus open contiguous with the bar (never a free-floating Popover); reuse the quick-settings-menu CSS class. Open on the same output as the pill; slide direction follows the bar edge; one menu at a time per frame; edge overflow clamps and scrolls, never clips.

nodes.label: revealer-row pattern
nodes.rationale: The house style for 'status + expand to details/devices': action button, content (label or slider+%), reveal chevron. Reuse the component, don't rebuild the row.

nodes.label: boxed-list primitive (.ok-boxed-list)
nodes.rationale: A group of related rows in ONE card (Adwaita .boxed-list): rows square-cornered, separated by 1px hairlines, first/last inherit card corners. A card-in-card is wrong; rows already inside a card keep the flat device-row treatment.

nodes.label: panel archetype (spacious surfaces)
nodes.rationale: The roomier sibling of a menu: own header (reusable PanelHeader), generous padding, soft elevation, segmented control, pill search. Still a layer-shell menu, not a detached window. Reference implementation: the clipboard menu + the dashboard.

nodes.label: bar -> menu wiring checklist (11 touch-points)
nodes.rationale: Adding a pill that opens its own menu touches 11 points in order (bar_widgets, bar.rs, menu.rs, menu_widgets, builder.rs, mshell-config menu_widgets/config, frame.rs, relm_app.rs, ipc.rs, mshellctl); miss one and it silently routes wrong or won't build. Copy bluetooth/cpu_dashboard/audio_dashboard exactly.

nodes.label: design lint quality gate (L1-L9)
nodes.rationale: A rule no one checks is a suggestion: grep-checkable invariants wired into CI. No hardcoded hex (L1), no raw px for spacing/radius/font >=4 (L2), --radius-widget/window only on bar/frame (L3), no literal motion duration (L4), no Popover as a bar widget primary surface (L5), no empty css class (L6), no GtkDragSource/DropTarget for ListBox reorder (L7). Run cargo fmt --check un-piped (a pipe masks the failure).

nodes.label: reorder DnD standard (reorder_dnd.rs)
nodes.rationale: GestureDrag not GTK DnD (a GtkListBox swallows DnD before the row sees it); a fixed STEP_PX (32) threshold not the row's own height (tall rows would round every drag to zero); >=28px .reorder-grip; .dragging feedback within a frame; keep the up/down keyboard buttons.

nodes.label: control-center tile anatomy
nodes.rationale: The canonical toggle (control_center/tile.rs): Normal/Expandable/Small shapes; active = whole-tile --primary fill (GNOME quick-settings, not a coloured chip) that animates. Keep the live subtitle current via set_subtitle.

nodes.label: matugen CSS-variable token system
nodes.rationale: All colours come from matugen at runtime (:root overridden by the style manager); never hardcode a hex - reference the variable so the widget re-themes with the wallpaper. --surface-opacity scales a surface's alpha only (never recolours).

nodes.label: surface decision tree (pill/menu/panel/tile/page)
nodes.rationale: Answer once up front (picking wrong is expensive): Settings page > dashboard tile > panel > menu > bar pill; first match wins. When a surface starts doing another's job, split it. Each surface type owns exactly one interaction role.

hyperedges.label: mshell coherent design system (tokens + severity + tint + surfaces)
